# <Plan title — a theme, not a version>

Status: queued

<!-- Copy this file to docs/plans/<theme>.md to queue a plan. When work starts,
     git mv it into active/ and rename it to carry the milestone (e.g.
     active/v1.2.0-<theme>.md); set Status: active. When it ships, git mv it to
     completed/ with Verification filled in.
     See README.md for the lifecycle, naming, and the markdown-link honesty rule. -->

## Objective

<The outcome in one paragraph: what will be true when this is done.>

## Value

<Why this, why now, why in this order. The cost of not doing it.>

## Scope

Each deliverable is a checklist item with an acceptance criterion —
the observable fact that proves it done.

1. **<Deliverable>** — <what it is>.
   *Acceptance: <the command output, file state, or behavior that proves it>.*
2. **<Deliverable>** — <what it is>.
   *Acceptance: <...>.*

## Out of scope

<What is deliberately deferred, and where it belongs instead — so it isn't
relitigated mid-execution.>

## Dependencies

<What must exist or merge first. "None" is a valid answer.>

## Verification

<The end-to-end evidence that will prove completion: the commands to run, the
tests/evals that must pass, the behavior to observe. Not "tests pass" — name
them.>

## Progress

<Dated running log, newest first. One entry per working session.>

- <YYYY-MM-DD> — Scoped.

## Decisions

<Dated choices and their why, so a resuming session doesn't reopen them.>

- <YYYY-MM-DD> — <decision> because <reason>.

## Next action

<The single next step a resuming session takes. Keep it to one concrete move.>
